title: Variation 1: First-time conversation
dialogue:
A: Hi! How's it going?
B: Hi! It’s going well. How about you?
A: I’m good, thank you. I’m Alex.
B: Nice to meet you, Alex. I’m Sam.
A: Nice to meet you too, Sam.
B: What do you do?
A: I work in a library. What about you?
B: I am a teacher.
A: That’s great! What do you teach?
B: I teach math.

title: Variation 2: Problem-solving discussion
dialogue:
A: Hi, can you help me?
B: Sure! What do you need?
A: I need help with my homework.
B: What subject?
A: Math. I don’t understand this problem.
B: Let me see.
A: Here it is.
B: Oh, I see. It is easy!
A: Really? Can you explain it?
B: Yes! First, we add these numbers.

title: Variation 3: Quick decision under time pressure
dialogue:
A: What should we do for lunch?
B: I don’t know. We have 10 minutes.
A: Let’s decide quickly.
B: Okay. Do you want pizza?
A: Yes, I like pizza.
B: Great! Where is the pizza place?
A: It’s near here. Let’s go!
B: Good idea! Let’s hurry.

title: Variation 4: Polite disagreement
dialogue:
A: I think we should go to the park.
B: I see your point, but I prefer the beach.
A: The beach is nice, but it is far.
B: Yes, but it is fun.
A: I think the park is easier.
B: Okay, maybe we can go to the park first.
A: That sounds good! Let’s do that.

title: Variation 5: Making a request
dialogue:
A: Excuse me, could you please help me?
B: Yes, what do you need?
A: Can I have a glass of water?
B: Of course! Here you go.
A: Thank you! Also, can I have a menu?
B: Sure! Here is the menu.
A: Thanks again!

title: Variation 6: Follow-up after a misunderstanding
dialogue:
A: Hi! I didn’t understand your message.
B: Oh, I’m sorry! Can you repeat that?
A: Yes, please. What time is the meeting?
B: The meeting is at 3 PM.
A: Thank you! I thought it was at 2 PM.
B: No problem! It’s at 3 PM.

title: Variation 7: Planning for tomorrow
dialogue:
A: What are you doing tomorrow?
B: I am free. What about you?
A: I want to go to the movies.
B: Sounds fun! What time?
A: Let’s meet at 5 PM.
B: Where should we meet?
A: Let’s meet at the café.
B: Great! See you at the café!

title: Variation 8: Asking for advice
dialogue:
A: Hi! I need your advice.
B: Sure! What do you think?
A: Should I buy this book?
B: Yes, it is very good.
A: Really? I like reading.
B: You will enjoy it!
A: Thank you for your advice!

title: Variation 9: Comparing two options
dialogue:
A: I have two shirts. Which one is better?
B: Let me see. I like the blue one.
A: Really? I like the red one more.
B: The blue one is nice for summer.
A: Yes, and it is cool.
B: But the red one is good for winter.
A: True! I will wear the blue one today.

title: Variation 10: Wrapping up and next steps
dialogue:
A: Let’s summarize our plan.
B: Okay! We will meet at 3 PM.
A: Yes, and we will go to the park.
B: Good! Next, we will have lunch.
A: Yes, lunch is important!
B: After lunch, we can play games.
A: That sounds fun! See you tomorrow!
